phase3_findings.description: coverage guard masked by declared exec edges — a silent import/source scan gap leaves the helper 'classified' via its exec edge
exec forward-verification satisfied by a comment/docstring mention of the token (git/gh/jq appear in comments) — stale declaration not caught
prefix-collision (.devflow/vendor/devflowX/...) and repo-owned-exec positive control untested
resolves_beneath_vendor accepts an absolute target via pathlib reset (latent, unreachable today)

fix_decisions.note: coverage guard now per-derived-scan-kind: a .py entry point with zero import edges is caught (AST scan gap); .sh floor stays any-edge since a helper may source nothing (config-get.sh)

_exec_target_present strips line comments and accepts DEVFLOW_<TOOL> evidence; live guard stays green (real code evidence exists for every declared external edge). Residual docstring-only gap disclosed in module docstring, same tradeoff as exec reverse-drift
added prefix-collision, absolute-target, repo-owned-exec positive control, coverage py-import-gap, and comment-only-token rejection tests (18 -> 24 AC5 assertions)
resolves_beneath_vendor rejects an absolute repo_rel up front

notes: review-and-fix engine driven inline; Phase-3 correctness roster (code-reviewer, silent-failure-hunter, pr-test-analyzer) dispatched over the branch diff. No Critical/Important correctness defect in shipped behavior; the two Important fail-open findings were in the guard's own strength and are fixed. Full inline observability/shadow machinery degraded under the sandbox's redirect/mktemp denials; recorded via workpad reflections.
